text: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) is the standard for sending email across the Internet. It uses TCP port 25 (or 587 for submission). Email clients submit mail; MTAs relay it between servers. SMTP is text-based and uses `MAIL FROM`, `RCPT TO`, `DATA` commands.
SMTP extensions (ESMTP): STARTTLS (encryption), 8BITMIME (non-ASCII), SIZE (message size declaration).
IMAP/POP3 are for receiving (reading) email — SMTP is send-only.
